tasks.name: review-report-agent
tasks.task:
source_report: ~/docs/knowledge-review-agent.md
knowledge_dir: knowledge/agent
review_output: ~/docs/review/knowledge-review-agent.md

Read source_report. For each finding, verify by reading the actual files in knowledge_dir:
1. Relocation suggestions: Is the suggested target directory genuinely more appropriate? Read both the file and representative files in the suggested target to confirm.
2. Duplicate candidates: Read both files being compared. Is the overlap real and significant enough to warrant action?
3. Skill candidates: Does the described workflow actually match the Skill format (.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md)?

If any findings are inaccurate, overstated, or lack sufficient justification, write review_output listing only those issues with brief reasoning. If all findings are sound, do NOT create review_output.

tasks.name: review-report-summary
tasks.task:
source_report: ~/docs/knowledge-review-summary.md
review_output: ~/docs/review/knowledge-review-summary.md

Read source_report and all per-directory reports in ~/docs/knowledge-review-*.md (excluding summary). Verify:
1. Are the top relocation suggestions consistent with what the per-directory reports actually found?
2. Are the confirmed cross-directory duplicates genuinely supported by both reports involved?
3. Are the top skill candidates actually the highest-value ones across all directories?
4. Are there important patterns in the per-directory reports that the summary missed?

If any of the above reveal inaccuracies, omissions, or misrepresentations, write review_output listing only those issues. If the summary accurately reflects the per-directory reports, do NOT create review_output.
